The Top 100

Researchers active on the Riemann hypothesis and the zeros of zeta and L-functions

Table Details

Columns

  • # overall rank (lower is better)
  • Name the researcher
  • Institution current affiliation
  • Ctry country
  • aX arXiv rank
  • OA OpenAlex rank
  • zM zbMATH rank
  • h h-index (citation impact)
  • Source databases that list them
  • Frm / To first and last active year

How it works. arXiv, OpenAlex and zbMATH each rank a researcher; the three ranks merge into one, weighted toward each person's strongest. Recent work counts more. Lower is better. Click any header to sort. A rank in [brackets] is an estimate, not measured. Full method.

Source column legend

The Source column records which pipeline ranked each researcher directly. Where a pipeline did not rank someone, the rank shown for that pipeline is an interpolated estimate (in [square brackets]).

  • all_three: ranked directly by arXiv, OpenAlex, and zbMATH. The unanimous core.
  • arx+oa: ranked by arXiv and OpenAlex, but not zbMATH.
  • arx+zb: ranked by arXiv and zbMATH, but not OpenAlex.
  • oa+zb: ranked by OpenAlex and zbMATH, but not arXiv.
  • arxiv_only: ranked only by the arXiv pipeline.
  • oa_only: ranked only by OpenAlex. Often senior figures who publish in journals and do not post preprints.
  • zb_only: ranked only by zbMATH. RH is an arXiv-heavy field, so most of the list is ranked from arXiv preprint output, with OpenAlex contributing where it overlaps.
SourceCount
all_three46
zb_only18
oa_only12
arx+zb9
arx+oa8
arxiv_only4
oa+zb3

Limitations

This list reflects publication output and citation network density, not subjective importance. A starting point, not a verdict.

Maintained by Steve Hubbard (admin@wwirh.org). Data sources: arXiv, OpenAlex, zbMATH Open, Mathematics Genealogy Project.

Last built July 2026.
